title: AcyclicHardInterventionTopologicalOrder
lean_code_with_comments:
def extOrder (lt : Node → Node → Prop) :
    IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop
  | .intCopy w1, .intCopy w2 => lt w1 w2
  | .intCopy _,  .unsplit _  => True
  | .unsplit _,  .intCopy _  => False
  | .unsplit u1, .unsplit u2 => lt u1 u2

def restrictOrder (lt' : IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop) :
    Node → Node → Prop :=
  fun v1 v2 => lt' (.unsplit v1) (.unsplit v2)

theorem extAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    (hAcyc : G.IsAcyclic) :
    (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsAcyclic
-- claim_3_13 -- end statement
  := by
  -- TeX proof: tex/claim_3_13_proof_AcyclicHardInterventionTopologicalOrder.tex
  -- Corollary derivation from sub-claim (c) via `claim_3_2`'s
  -- `acyclic_iff_topological_order`: extract a topological order of `G`
  -- via `(claim_3_2).mp`, lift to one of the extension via
  -- `aux_extTopologicalOrder`, conclude via `(claim_3_2).mpr`.  Mirrors
  -- `swigAcyclic`'s structure in `SwigAcyclic.lean`.
  obtain ⟨lt, hlt⟩ := (acyclic_iff_topological_order G).mp hAcyc
  exact (acyclic_iff_topological_order (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW)).mpr
    ⟨extOrder lt, aux_extTopologicalOrder G W hW lt hlt⟩

theorem extRestrictsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    (lt' : IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop)
    (hlt' : (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsTopologicalOrder lt') :
    G.IsTopologicalOrder (restrictOrder lt')
-- claim_3_13 -- end statement
  := by
  -- TeX proof: tex/claim_3_13_proof_AcyclicHardInterventionTopologicalOrder.tex
  -- Verify each of the four `IsTopologicalOrder` clauses for
  -- `restrictOrder lt'` on `G` by pulling the corresponding clause for
  -- `lt'` on the extension back through `.unsplit` via
  -- `unsplit_mem_ext` (membership lift) and the lifted-edge clause of
  -- `def_3_13` item iii (parent-precedence lift).
  obtain ⟨⟨h_irrefl', h_trans', h_tri'⟩, h_pa'⟩ := hlt'
  refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
  · -- Irreflexivity
    intro v hv hlt_vv
    exact h_irrefl' (.unsplit v) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv) hlt_vv
  · -- Transitivity
    intro u hu v hv w hw huv hvw
    exact h_trans' (.unsplit u) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hu)
      (.unsplit v) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv)
      (.unsplit w) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hw) huv hvw
  · -- Trichotomy
    intro v hv w hw
    rcases h_tri' (.unsplit v) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv)
        (.unsplit w) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hw)
      with hlt | heq | hlt
    · left; exact hlt
    · right; left; exact IntExtNode.unsplit.inj heq
    · right; right; exact hlt
  · -- Parent precedence
    intro v w h_pa_vw
    obtain ⟨hv_mem, hvw_E⟩ := h_pa_vw
    refine h_pa' (.unsplit v) (.unsplit w) ⟨unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv_mem, ?_⟩
    -- goal: (.unsplit v, .unsplit w) ∈ (extension).E
    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    exact Finset.mem_image.mpr ⟨(v, w), hvw_E, rfl⟩

theorem extExtendsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    (lt : Node → Node → Prop) (hlt : G.IsTopologicalOrder lt) :
    (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsTopologicalOrder (extOrder lt)
-- claim_3_13 -- end statement
  := by
  -- TeX proof: tex/claim_3_13_proof_AcyclicHardInterventionTopologicalOrder.tex
  -- One-line wrapper around the private workhorse `aux_extTopologicalOrder`.
  -- Mirrors `swigTopologicalOrder`'s wrapper in `SwigAcyclic.lean`.
  exact aux_extTopologicalOrder G W hW lt hlt
lean_code_without_comments:
def extOrder (lt : Node → Node → Prop) :
    IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop
  | .intCopy w1, .intCopy w2 => lt w1 w2
  | .intCopy _,  .unsplit _  => True
  | .unsplit _,  .intCopy _  => False
  | .unsplit u1, .unsplit u2 => lt u1 u2

def restrictOrder (lt' : IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop) :
    Node → Node → Prop :=
  fun v1 v2 => lt' (.unsplit v1) (.unsplit v2)

theorem extAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    (hAcyc : G.IsAcyclic) :
    (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsAcyclic

  := by

  obtain ⟨lt, hlt⟩ := (acyclic_iff_topological_order G).mp hAcyc
  exact (acyclic_iff_topological_order (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW)).mpr
    ⟨extOrder lt, aux_extTopologicalOrder G W hW lt hlt⟩

theorem extRestrictsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    (lt' : IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop)
    (hlt' : (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsTopologicalOrder lt') :
    G.IsTopologicalOrder (restrictOrder lt')

  := by

  obtain ⟨⟨h_irrefl', h_trans', h_tri'⟩, h_pa'⟩ := hlt'
  refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩, ?_⟩
  ·
    intro v hv hlt_vv
    exact h_irrefl' (.unsplit v) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv) hlt_vv
  ·
    intro u hu v hv w hw huv hvw
    exact h_trans' (.unsplit u) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hu)
      (.unsplit v) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv)
      (.unsplit w) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hw) huv hvw
  ·
    intro v hv w hw
    rcases h_tri' (.unsplit v) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv)
        (.unsplit w) (unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hw)
      with hlt | heq | hlt
    · left; exact hlt
    · right; left; exact IntExtNode.unsplit.inj heq
    · right; right; exact hlt
  ·
    intro v w h_pa_vw
    obtain ⟨hv_mem, hvw_E⟩ := h_pa_vw
    refine h_pa' (.unsplit v) (.unsplit w) ⟨unsplit_mem_ext (hW := hW) hv_mem, ?_⟩

    refine Finset.mem_union_left _ ?_
    exact Finset.mem_image.mpr ⟨(v, w), hvw_E, rfl⟩

theorem extExtendsTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V)
    (lt : Node → Node → Prop) (hlt : G.IsTopologicalOrder lt) :
    (G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsTopologicalOrder (extOrder lt)

  := by

  exact aux_extTopologicalOrder G W hW lt hlt
lean_explanation:
This file formalises the LN's remark that the hard-intervention extension $G_{\doit(I_W)}$ preserves acyclicity and that the topological orders of $G$ and of $G_{\doit(I_W)}$ are in mutual correspondence along the inclusion of original nodes. The ambient vertex type is `Node : Type*` with a `[DecidableEq Node]` instance, both inherited verbatim from the CDMG definition (`def_3_1`). The extension lives on the carrier `IntExtNode Node` from `def_3_13`, a two-constructor tagged-sum inductive: `.unsplit v` embeds an original node $v \in J \cup V$ into the extended carrier, and `.intCopy w` introduces a fresh intervention node $I_w$ for each $w \in W \setminus J$. The LN-side canonical inclusion $\iota : J \cup V \hookrightarrow J_{\doit(I_W)} \cup V_{\doit(I_W)}$ is realised constructor-wise as `.unsplit`.

Two short helpers encode the carrier-change. `extOrder lt : IntExtNode Node → IntExtNode Node → Prop` lifts a strict relation `lt` on `Node` to the extended carrier by four-case analysis on the two constructors, placing every `.intCopy` strictly before every `.unsplit` and delegating within each tag-class to `lt` on the underlying base nodes. Symmetrically, `restrictOrder lt' : Node → Node → Prop` pulls a relation on the extended carrier back along `.unsplit`, realising the LN's $v_1 <_G v_2 \;:\Leftrightarrow\; \iota(v_1) \prec \iota(v_2)$ as a one-line lambda.

The remark is then stated as three separate theorems. `extAcyclic` takes a CDMG `G`, a subset $W \subseteq G.J \cup G.V$ (encoded as a `Finset Node` plus a subset-witness `hW`), and the hypothesis `G.IsAcyclic`, and concludes `(G.extendingCDMGsWith W hW).IsAcyclic`. `extRestrictsTopologicalOrder` takes an `lt'` on the extension together with a proof that it is a topological order, and concludes that `restrictOrder lt'` is a topological order on `G`. `extExtendsTopologicalOrder` takes a topological order `lt` on `G` and concludes that the explicit witness `extOrder lt` is a topological order of the extension. The non-trivial Mathlib idioms in play are `Finset` operations (`Finset.image`, `Finset.mem_union`, `Finset.mem_sdiff`) used by the private edge-set-slicing helpers, and the dot-notation `G.IsTopologicalOrder` projecting through the bundled `IsTotalOrder ∧ parent-precedence` predicate of `def_3_8`.
design_choices:
**Three separate theorems rather than one bundled conjunction.** The LN presents (a), (b), and (c) inside a single `\begin{Rem}`, but the three sub-claims have *different* hypotheses and live on *different* carriers: (a) takes acyclicity of `G` and concludes a property of the extension; (b) takes a relation `lt'` on `IntExtNode Node` and produces a relation on `Node`; (c) takes a relation `lt` on `Node` and produces one on `IntExtNode Node`. Bundling them would force every consumer to project through tuple components for the part they need, or to pass hypotheses they don't use. Three self-contained theorems keep each statement focused, matching the analogous splits in `SplitTopologicalOrder.lean` and `SwigAcyclic.lean`.

**Case-analysis encoding of `extOrder`, not a $\mathbb{Q}$-arithmetic detour.** The four-case definition is a direct structural transcription of the LN's piecewise rule. A common alternative — embedding both tag-classes into $\mathbb{Q}$ and ordering by the embedded representatives — was rejected because it imposes a rational-arithmetic detour with no payoff. Reading off the order's clauses by `cases` on the two constructors gives the cleanest tactic shape and the lex-on-tag-then-base structure becomes structurally visible.

**Lex orientation `.intCopy` below `.unsplit`.** Forced by parent-precedence: every fresh edge `(.intCopy w, .unsplit w)` from `def_3_13` requires its source to precede its target under any topological order of the extension. The second case of `extOrder` discharges this with `True` by construction; the reverse orientation would break the parent-precedence proof at the fresh-edge sub-case.

**The order among the fresh intervention nodes is a design choice.** The LN deliberately leaves the relative order among the fresh $I_w$ unspecified, because the fresh nodes are pairwise non-adjacent in the extension, so any strict total order on them respects parent-precedence. We pick `extOrder lt (.intCopy w₁) (.intCopy w₂) := lt w₁ w₂`, reusing the underlying `lt`'s trichotomy on $w_1, w_2 \in W \setminus J \subseteq V$ for free, rather than inventing an arbitrary enumeration of $W \setminus J$.

**No acyclicity hypothesis on sub-claims (b) and (c).** Acyclicity of `G` is load-bearing only for (a); the parent-precedence and order-restriction arguments of (b) and (c) transport pointwise via `.unsplit`'s constructor injectivity and the lifted-edge clause of the extension's edge-set-builder, without consulting acyclicity. Keeping each signature minimal — rather than LN-faithfully bundling acyclicity over all three sub-claims — means consumers that have acyclicity in scope can simply ignore it.

**Relations as bare `Prop`-valued binary relations, not `LT` typeclasses.** Mirrors `def_3_8`'s `IsTopologicalOrder` interface: the LN universally quantifies over `<_G` and $\prec$. A typeclass `[LT (IntExtNode Node)]` would force exactly one canonical order per carrier and collide with the explicit external parameterisation the existential and universal directions both demand.
